identify what you're struggling with about the way things are now

being in integrity with yourself means showing up in a way where shitty dynamics can’t be maintained

shame doesn't stop bad behaviour, it enables & perpetuates it

I am my own permission slip; I give myself permission; I set myself free; I am free

and it feels good :)

after many years of (bless me) living in & with crippling shame & self-hatred, it feels really good to be in a place where I’m not available for that. it just isn’t how I feel about myself anymore, let alone my life choices; I don’t see any reason for it

something that has become v clear to me over the years is how few people are living lives that are genuinely true to themselves & their soul’s calling. what I have observed is that these same people tend to judge others & project their fears & dissatisfaction outward. I don't…

on a fundamental level, I am not living my life for other people, which also means that I am not living my life for anyone else’s approval. what matters to me is that my life feels good for me, and that I continue to take responsibility for my actions

I am no longer afraid of “making ppl feel uncomfortable by being me” bc any discomfort they feel no longer threatens my self-concept or makes me feel uncomfortable to be around; I can sit w it, I can be w it, I can welcome it, and I can see it for what it is—love in another form

I am at a point in my life where I am old & wise enough to be unavailable for silencing or being less of myself to soothe others’ superficial comfort. I say & do this not to be superfluously loud or showy, but simply to live my own principles & allow myself to exist as I am, too

it is not my intention to trigger ppl by existing. it is my intention to be a force for beauty, love & goodness in the world, and in that honour & be true to myself. I feel blessed to have developed the kind of energetic constitution that allows me to be comfortable w being bold

what you choose to think of me, if you think anything at all, is directly related to what you think—and like/dislike/own/disown—about yourself & your life, especially if you don't know me well or at all as a person, and I feel secure in that

on a personal level, I notice I am much more comfortable with being misunderstood/judged when I have my cards on the table face-up, plainly, bc it becomes abundantly clear that what you choose to see there (if it isn't “just” the cards) is yours, not mine—and little to do with me

when it comes to legibility, what I am learning is it is much worse to be afraid of the consequences of telling the/living your truth than it is to actually do it— if only bc allowing yourself to finally be seen & known for who you are & what’s important to you is worth e/t else

it is true that I am significantly more comfortable with talking about & navigating taboo/triggering topics than the average person; it is also true that I have significant expertise in this area because I’ve put the reps in to gain it. this isn't luck or even talent; it’s will

a good question to ask when you're in a shame hot potato: 

“what is making the other people feel ashamed getting or giving you?”
getting out of the shame hot potato requires you to drop the shame hot potato & feel the shame you've been avoiding

the intellectual side of the shame hot potato: 

defensiveness; trying to justify & argue for your behaviour

trying to show someone else that & how they're wrong is not a very effective strategy for creating what you actually want with them, ~whatever that might be. this is especially true if they are someone you genuinely care about

from the other person’s point of view, they are always right

from a personal & interpersonal standpoint, it is useful to seek to understand how what the other person is saying is true for them if you want to have a good (positive-feeling, generative) relationship with them
trying to figure out who did xyz wrong (or who *is* wrong) won’t make xyz right
on a nervous system level, often the only thing you have to do to make someone feel like they’re bad is get angry at them

what the shame hot potato looks like in practice:

1. anger & the removal of love
2. aggression & passive-aggression

(the above = two ways of saying the same thing)

the personal is political is relational is global:

countries that are at war with one another are in some way defensive, ashamed & not in full alignment with who they are

aka, everyone is making compromises & feels like shit

(being at war is clearly a sub-optimal position)

defensiveness = a key tell of being in a shame hot potato

(what are you trying to avoid feeling & seeing yourself as?)

you have to buy into the way that another party is attempting to shame you to become defensive around it

(aka, some part of you both has to believe & care about the thing to engage from a triggered & reactive place)

more shame hot potato tells:

I am no longer available for pretending to be something, someone or some way I’m not in the hope of appealing to more people who realistically are unlikely to appeal to me

my love is my love. my heart is my heart. my life is my life. I magnetise people who resonate with the real me, appreciate who I am & what I have to give and want to get to know me & be in my life. I repel anyone I wouldn’t naturally vibe with. that’s life

I am not fearless. I can be hurt, I can be misunderstood, and both feel painful for me when they happen bc I deeply care about human connection. and, it’s the kind of pain I choose & will keep on choosing bc I don’t need to prove how powerful I am to be or feel powerful anymore

in the past, I’ve navigated this topic with an energy of gung-ho strength, relentlessness & defiance because it’s what I’ve needed— and had the skills & capacity—to do to be able to rise above the noise and stay (or become more) true to myself & my path. but I’m different now

there is a reality that being judged & shamed by others hurts, and I don't want to become impervious to that because I think it’s at the core of what makes us prosocial creatures as humans. to care about what others think is a beautiful thing. and, which others really matter?

more & more in my life, I’m seeing that others’ potential or actual judgements of me wouldn't touch me if a part of me didn't buy into their story too—or fear losing their approval. this is exactly the way of being I’m here to excavate & heal so I can be sovereign & live my truth

I’ve been so aware of how this kind of self-expression can be perceived & judged—and wary of “unwittingly turning people I’d otherwise be compatible with off”, esp wrt potential life partners—that I’ve fallen into exactly the trap I talk about often: muzzling & shaming myself

one way I’ve dimmed my light for a long time is by suppressing my desire to publicly express & explore my love for (and deep love with) multiple people at once. this isn't just about my intimate (sexual) relationships, but my overall capacity, desire & appetite to love & be loved
